content: SemVer v1.0 stabilization: Before 1.0.0 anything can change. After 1.0.0 all semver rules apply strictly. Moving from alpha (x.y.z-alpha) to v1.0.0 requires at minimum one deprecation cycle — issue a minor release with deprecations before removing in major. Breaking changes include: TypeScript type changes (narrowing/widening counts), removing supported Node.js versions, updating peer deps (requires major bump), bug fix that removes behavior users relied on. Pre-release labels (alpha.1, alpha.2) must be unique to avoid overwrites. v1.0 must have complete stable public API documented.
